description: You are an Operations Research Analyst at an airline auditing pilot schedules for FAA Part 117 fatigue rule violations using the AVIATION_AUDIT schema in Oracle (Host=localhost, Port=1521, Service=XEPDB1, User=aviation_audit, Password=Flight2024). Create a view named CREW_REST_PERIODS_VW that calculates continuous rest hours between duties (checkout to next checkin) using a LEAD window function partitioned by crew_id; columns must include crew_id, flight_id (from current_flight_id), next_flight_id, check_out_time, next_check_in_time, and rest_hours. Create a view named ROLLING_FLIGHT_HOURS_VW to track cumulative 28-day flight block hours per crew member using a window function with a range of 28 preceding days; columns must include crew_id, flight_id, actual_dep, flight_hours, and rolling_28d_hours. Create a table named FAA_VIOLATION_REPORT with columns for violation_id, crew_id, violation_type, flight_id, event_date, and details. Develop and execute a PL/SQL procedure named PROC_AUDIT_VIOLATIONS that truncates the report table and populates it with 'INSUFFICIENT_REST' (rest < 10 hours, using next_check_in_time as event_date) and 'EXCEEDED_28D_LIMIT' (rolling 28d > 100 hours, using actual_dep as event_date) violations. Export the populated FAA_VIOLATION_REPORT table to /home/ga/Documents/faa_audit_report.csv.
